g.text: 1. All dimensions in inches unless noted
2. Bridge: pressure-treated lumber or rot-resistant species (cedar, locust)
3. Pylons: 6x6 PT posts driven/buried min 24" into hillside with compacted gravel
4. All hardware: hot-dipped galvanized or stainless steel
5. Attach non-slip mesh (chicken wire) to bridge deck surface
6. Lander: compact fill in 6" lifts with plate compactor
7. Use mineral soil only (clay/gravel mix, no organics)
8. Landing slope (40.0°) provides gradual deceleration — no kicker at base
9. Rider must have clear sight line from bridge to landing zone
10. ALWAYS provide a bypass / roll-around for this feature
11. Total vertical drop from deck to lander peak: 11'-0" — expert-level feature
12. Log crib wall: 8.0" dia. logs, peeled, stacked horizontally, pinned with #4 rebar
13. Pin each log course to the one below — drive 24" rebar through pre-drilled holes
14. Deadman tie-backs every 2-3 courses extending 4 ft into compacted fill behind wall
15. Place 3/4" crushed gravel behind crib wall with filter fabric for drainage
16. Bottom course of logs set on compacted gravel pad, first rebar pins driven into native soil
Pin logs together + anchor to ground
Between gravel and fill behind crib

svg.text: Option E — 40° Lander, 4ft Case Pad, 12ft Sweet Spot
Vertical drop to lander: 11'-0" | Drop to ground: 15'-6"
Sweet spot: 12'-0" horiz | Lander: 4'-6" tall | Landing: 40.0°
